name: Margherita Pizza
description: A classic Neapolitan pizza topped with San Marzano tomatoes, fresh mozzarella, basil, and olive oil on a thin crust.
funFact: Named after Queen Margherita of Savoy who visited Naples in 1889.

name: Pad Thai
description: Stir-fried rice noodles with shrimp, tofu, eggs, bean sprouts, and tamarind sauce, topped with crushed peanuts and lime.
funFact: Pad Thai was promoted as a national dish during WWII when rice was scarce and noodles were encouraged.

name: Tacos al Pastor
description: Marinated pork cooked on a vertical spit, served on corn tortillas with pineapple, onion, cilantro, and salsa.
funFact: Al Pastor was introduced by Lebanese immigrants to Mexico in the early 1900s, inspired by shawarma.

name: Sushi
description: Vinegared rice paired with fresh raw fish, seafood, and vegetables, often wrapped in nori seaweed.
funFact: Sushi originally began as a method of preserving fish in fermented rice during the 2nd century.

name: Butter Chicken
description: Tender chicken pieces in a rich, creamy tomato-based curry with butter, cream, and aromatic spices.
funFact: Butter chicken was invented by accident at Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi in the 1950s.

name: Croissant
description: A buttery, flaky, crescent-shaped pastry made from laminated yeast-leavened dough.
funFact: The croissant actually originated from the Austrian kipferl and was brought to France by Marie Antoinette.

name: Paella
description: Saffron-infused rice dish with seafood, chicken, rabbit, green beans, and bell peppers, cooked in a wide shallow pan.
funFact: Authentic paella is traditionally cooked over an open fire with orange and pine branches for smoky flavor.

name: Peking Duck
description: Roasted duck with crispy skin, served with thin pancakes, spring onions, cucumber, and sweet hoisin sauce.
funFact: Peking duck has been a royal dish since the Yuan Dynasty (1271-1368).

name: Shawarma
description: Seasoned meat slow-roasted on a vertical spit, thinly sliced and served in pita with tahini, pickles, and vegetables.
funFact: The word 'shawarma' comes from the Turkish word 'çevirme', meaning 'turning'.

name: Bibimbap
description: A bowl of warm rice topped with sautéed vegetables, gochujang chili paste, soy sauce, sliced meat, and a fried egg.
funFact: Bibimbap originated as a meal for farmers during harvest season, mixing leftovers into one bowl.

name: Feijoada
description: A hearty black bean stew with pork, beef, and smoked sausages, traditionally served with rice, collard greens, and orange slices.
funFact: Feijoada is traditionally served on Wednesdays and Saturdays in Brazil.

name: Moussaka
description: Layered casserole of eggplant, minced meat, and creamy béchamel sauce, baked until golden and bubbly.
funFact: The word 'moussaka' is derived from the Arabic 'musaqqa'a', meaning 'chilled'.
